'''TK-875''' was a [[clone stormtrooper]] who served in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], he was present on the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] when a group of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] operatives infiltrated the [[Space station/Legends|station]] to [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|rescue]] one of its [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. Following the rescue, within the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] [[Central Core Shaft]], the Princess, along with another member of the group, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], found themselves behind a [[Blast door/Legends|blast door]] leading to a shaft. Skywalker had unknowingly disabled the controls to a bridge meant to extend across the shaft, and as the two were trapped on a narrow overhang, TK-875 was part of a trio of stormtroopers who exchanged [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire with them from a higher overhang across the shaft. Eventually, Skywalker and Organa managed to swing across the shaft with the use of a [[Grappling hook/Legends|grappling hook]], evading TK-875's blaster fire. The Alliance would later on [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destroy]] the Death Star over the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]].

==Biography==
The [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="CVD" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="SE" /> TK-875 was a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]]<ref name="card" /> who served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a conflict between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="ANH" /> By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].</ref> he<ref name="SE" /> was present aboard the Imperial [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]].<ref name="ANH" />

[[File:LukeLeiaDS1.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Luke Skywalker and Leia Organa Solo|TK-875 failed to prevent Leia Organa and Luke Skywalker (both pictured) from escaping the Central Core Shaft.]]
That [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="NEC" /> the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] was infiltrated by a group of soon-to-be operatives of the Alliance to Restore the Republic, consisting of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], as part of an [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|attempt]] to rescue one of the Death Star's [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. Skywalker escorted Organa to [[Docking Bay 327/Legends|Docking Bay 327]], the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bay that the group arrived in aboard the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. Going through the [[Space station/Legends|station's]] corridors on the way to the hangar, the pair were spotted by a stormtrooper patrol, and were forced to take cover behind a [[Blast door/Legends|blast door]] leading to the Death Star's [[Central Core Shaft]]. Attempting to prevent the stormtroopers from opening the door, Skywalker used his stolen stormtrooper [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11 blaster rifle]] to shoot the control panel by the door, unknowingly destroying the controls meant for a bridge to go across the chasm. As Skywalker and Organa were stuck on a narrow overhang, TK-875 was part of a trio of stormtroopers who arrived on a higher overhang across the pair, firing their E-11 [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]] at the Alliance operatives. Skywalker fired back, hitting one of the [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]], [[TK-119]], who fell down the shaft. As Skywalker prepared to swing across the chasm with the [[Grappling hook/Legends|grappling hook]] in his stolen stormtrooper [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belt]], Organa exchanged fire with TK-875 and the third stormtrooper, eventually [[Death/Legends|killing]] the latter. TK-875, the only one remaining, continued firing at the pair before they successfully swung across the shaft, evading his fire.<ref name="ANH" />

Organa and the Alliance operatives evacuated the Death Star to their [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. However, a tracking device was placed by the Empire on the ''Millennium Falcon'' to follow the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] to the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]]. The Death Star was [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|sent]] to destroy the base, but the Alliance's [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] launched a counterattack on the [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] itself. Skywalker, piloting a [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]], fired a pair of [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] which reached the Death Star's [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]] and detonated, obliterating the station.<ref name="ANH" />

==Personality and traits==
TK-875 wore full [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|stormtrooper armor]] and wielded an E-11 [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] [[Rifle/Legends|rifle]].<ref name="ANH" />

==Behind the scenes==
TK-875 first appeared in [[1977]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], the first film of the [[original trilogy]]. He was portrayed by an uncredited extra.<ref name="ANH" /> In [[2003]], he was first identified by name in a card for [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s [[Battle of Yavin (TCG)|''Battle of Yavin'' set]] of its [[Star Wars Trading Card Game|Trading Card Game]].<ref name="card" />
